item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents oppose the statement from the start and remain aligned against automatic society-over-individual priority. Agent A consistently rejects the statement but keeps a balance frame, allowing safety, welfare, mutual obligation, harmony, and social order within rights-protective limits. Agent B is more categorical by the final turn, calling the statement a shortcut to censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power. No visible reversal or cross-agent drift appears.
